lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A high price? That'll be nice for whoever can figure it out...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A high price? I guess I am somewhat of an expert on finding things for museums...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I should talk to the curator to find out some more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But this will have to be a job for another time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Curator Herminius
lines.text: Nilsal to you, iknami. Welcome to the Grand Museum. I hope your visit is a pleasant one. If you have any questions about Varlamore, I'm happy to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about that empty display?

lines.speaker: Curator Herminius
lines.text: Oh yes, Xerna's Diadem... Quite a shame, isn't it? Do you know anything about it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid not, but maybe I can help find it? I have managed to return stolen property to museums in the past.

lines.speaker: Curator Herminius
lines.text: That would certainly be most appreciated. The local guards said they'd investigate for us, but it's not proven to be a fast process so far.

lines.speaker: Curator Herminius
lines.text: I was starting to consider trying to find it myself, but I'm no detective. It doesn't seem to me like anything else is even out of place...

lines.speaker: Curator Herminius
lines.text: Perhaps you'll have better luck though. Maybe you could have a look around for any sort of clues and see if you can find anything wrong.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do that. Hopefully I can find something!

lines.speaker: Curator Herminius
lines.text: Thank you, iknami.

lines.speaker: Curator Herminius
lines.text: Nilsal to you, iknami. Welcome back to the Grand Museum. If you have any questions, I'm happy to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that empty display...

lines.speaker: Curator Herminius
lines.text: How are you getting on with your investigation?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not so well. I've not made much progress yet.

lines.speaker: Curator Herminius
lines.text: Well, you have our thanks for all your efforts in finding it. Thank you, iknami.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently someone was acting a bit odd around here recently. They left and headed towards the quetzals.

lines.speaker: Curator Herminius
lines.text: It sounds like you have a lead, iknami. You should follow it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it's looking like it could be quite the journey... I'll keep you updated!

lines.speaker: Curator Herminius
lines.text: Well, you have our thanks for all your efforts in finding it. Thank you, iknami.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just know I've almost got it! We'll have the diadem back in no time!

lines.speaker: Curator Herminius
lines.text: Well, you have our thanks for all your efforts in finding it. Thank you, iknami.

sections.title: Inpecting the empty display once more

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I'm going to find much more just lying around... Maybe I should try asking if anyone nearby saw anything.

sections.title: Talking to an academic, citizen or tourist that didn't see anything

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Academic
lines.text: Nilsal! Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering if you'd seen anything unusual around the museum lately?

lines.speaker: Academic
lines.text: No, I don't think so...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a shame. Well, thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: What sort of unusual?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking into the missing diadem display.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Oh... Sorry, but I don't think I can be much help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, thanks anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.text: Hi! Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering if you'd seen anything unusual around the museum lately?

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.text: Now you mention it, I did see somebody skulking around here recently... I thought they looked a little shifty at the time!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You did? Do you know where they went?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: They left through the front door and turned right, towards the quetzal landing site.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.text: They left through the front door and turned right, towards where they have those big birds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah that'll be the quetzals. Thanks!

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.text: Quetzals? You learn something new every day!

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: Nilsal, adventurer. Do you wish to return to Varrock?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen anybody suspicious around?

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: What kind of suspicious?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe someone carrying something out of the museum, perhaps?

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: Well let's see... Come to think of it, there was this one person carrying some sort of case...

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: He came along and asked if he could have a go with my bird.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He asked if he could have a go with your bird?

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: Yes, I thought it was quite strange. I told him no, and he made his way east, towards the Cothon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To the Cothon... Okay, I guess I'll go and see if I can find some more leads there. Thanks for the help!

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: Safe travels, iknami.

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: Nilsal, adventurer. Do you wish to return to Varrock?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen anybody suspicious around?

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: Just that man I already told you about. He headed east, towards the Cothon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, yes. Thanks for the help!

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: Safe travels, iknami.

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen a man with a case?

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Why do you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm looking for something and I think he might have it...

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: I don't think people would really like us talking all about their travels...

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Although, we could use a hand... Think you could do us a small favour?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure. What do you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As long as it is just the one favour...

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: We've been meaning to get our old sails stitched up, but haven't had a chance to get away and have it done...

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Hard to believe that Trader Stan had these clothes enchanted to re-stitch themselves so we can't ruin them, but the sails were too much to ask for! Some people's priorities, huh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose I can help with that.

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Perfect! There's a crafting store just down the road. They should be able to fix them up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I might need to make some space before I can take them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now.

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Then I don't know anything about a man with a case.

sections.title: Talking to a trader crewmember after declining doing the favour

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen a man with a case?

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: That depends. Are you ready to take us up on that favour?

sections.title: Talking to trader crewmember after agreeing to the favour

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, about that man with the case.

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Oh, yeah. You're meant to be getting our sails fixed up, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where was it that I should get them fixed?

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: That crafting store just down the road.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am, but I may have misplaced them...

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: You misplaced them right here. Here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am, but I think I might need to make some space before I can take them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am. Could I grab them now?

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Alright, here you go.

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: Nilsal. Here to do some crafting? I've got some supplies that might help you if so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was hoping for some help.

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: What sort of help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got these sails that need fixing up for the charter ship in the Cothon. The crew thought you could help.

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: Oh, in that case, I'll help you out, just so long as you can do me a small favour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go on then.

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: All I ask is that you tell the charter crew that Artima says thanks for the latest shipment they brought in!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, what a relief! I will be sure to pass on the message when I get back there.

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: Perfect. Now hand those sails over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: Let's see... Thread that through there...

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: Grab some of that, then pull that through...

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: Tie off the end there and...

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: There we have it! They might not look the prettiest, but they'll be sturdy enough for the job at least.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: Take care now iknami, and come by the shop whenever you need any crafting bits and bobs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe later.

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: Suit yourself, but those sails won't fix themselves you know.

sections.title: Talking to Artima after declining doing the favour

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: Nilsal. Here to do some crafting? I've got some supplies that might help you if so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So about those sails...

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: Are you ready to do that small favour I mentioned?

sections.title: Talking to Artima after losing the repaired sails

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: Nilsal. Here to do some crafting? I've got some supplies that might help you if so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So about those sails...

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: I already fixed them for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You did, but then I lost them.

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: Well, I'd be able to fix more if you had more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Luckily I do. Here you go.

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: Let's see... Thread that through there...

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: Grab some of that, then pull that through...

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: Tie off the end there and...

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: There we have it! They might not look the prettiest, but they'll be sturdy enough for the job at least.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Artima
lines.text: Take care now iknami, and come by the shop whenever you need any crafting bits and bobs.

sections.title: Talking to a trader crewmember with the sails

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, about that man with the case.

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Oh, yeah. You're meant to be getting our sails fixed up, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here they are!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, and Artima said thanks for the latest shipment.

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: That's great! Always nice to hear from a happy customer!

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Anyway, the man with the case. Grey-haired fella, right? Yeah, he came by. Asked if he could have a go with our boat. Told him that's not really how this works.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then what? Where did he go?

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Well, we explained how it does work and he then asked us to take him to Port Sarim...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Port Sarim? I see... Thanks.

sections.title: Talking to a trader crewmember after having returned the sails

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, about that man with the case.

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Oh, yeah. Grey-haired fella, right? Yeah, he came by. Asked if he could have a go with our boat. Told him that's now how this works.

sections.title: Talking to a trader crewmember in Port Sarim

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen a grey-haired man with a case?

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Yeah, I know the one. Came all the way from Fortis, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right! Do you know where he went?

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Wandered up north, I think. Said something about trying to get hold of some runes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see... Thanks for the help!

sections.title: Talking to a trader crewmember in Port Sarim again

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen a grey-haired man with a case?

lines.speaker: Trader Crewmember
lines.text: The one from Fortis? He wandered up north, I think. Said something about trying to get hold of some runes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see... Thanks for the help!

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Hello again. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen a grey-haired man with a case?

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Oh, the one with the monocle? Yes, he did come in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let me guess, he asked if he could have a go with your runes?

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: What? What does that even mean?

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: No, he came in looking to buy some runes, but he didn't really know what he was looking for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did he go?

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: I don't know. He took a look at my stock, but didn't see what he wanted. He then left.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you got any idea what runes he was looking for?

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: I did take some notes. It was driving me crazy trying to make sense of it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I take a look?

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Be my guest! Good luck making sense of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... I'd better free up some space first.

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Hello again. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About those notes...

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: What about them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any idea what he was hoping to do with the runes?

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Not a clue. Maybe you can figure out what he was trying to cast.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I take another look at them?

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Be my guest! Good luck making sense of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... I'd better free up some space first.

sections.title: Inspecting Betty's notes for the first time

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What could this mean?

sections.title: Talking to Betty again after visiting Curator Haig Halen

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Hello again. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I managed to decipher those notes.

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Oh really? Please do tell. It's been driving me crazy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems like he was trying to find the runes for the Varrock teleport spell.

lines.speaker: Betty
lines.text: Ah, of course. I'm not sure how I didn't figure that out!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Welcome to the museum of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for Xerna's Diadem.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Yes, awful news! I do hope you manage to find it!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Welcome to the museum of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for Xerna's Diadem.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Xerna's Diadem? How did you-

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Er... How did you come to hear about that? It's not something you hear of much around these parts!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it's missing from the Grand Museum in Fortis. I was hoping to try and find it...

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: What awful news! I'm not sure I can help you with that, though...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, if you say so...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well what do we have here? I wonder what our local curator has to say about this...

sections.title: Talking to Curator Haig Halen after finding the diadem

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Welcome to the museum of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found Xerna's Diadem...

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Ah...

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: So you found the diadem? That's... Er... That's wonderful news!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I'm sure that the Grand Museum will be thrilled to hear it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where I found it?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I... No, why would I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Be honest with me, curator!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I don't know what you're talking about!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can't just steal artefacts and claim them as your own for the museum!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Steal?! I would never! You need to get your facts straight!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The other museums will never trust you again!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: They'd understand my actions... I'm just trying to preserve history.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How would you feel if someone came in here and stole all your stuff?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Well, I'd feel terrible! Wait...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've ruined Varlamore's museum.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Well, as soon as they're ready to display it again, they can have it back!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just give it back already!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: No! You have no idea what you're asking me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if Varlamore finds out what you've done?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: They will find out! As soon as you stop bothering me, I plan to tell them myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you sleep at night?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Much better knowing that this valuable artefact is safe and sound!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought archaeology was cool. I didn't realise it was just thieving!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Archaeology is cool! This was just an exceptional circumstance...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You should be ashamed of yourself.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: There's nothing for me to be ashamed of! I merely did the best I could in a tough situation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A real historian would never steal!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Believe me, 'real' historians have done far worse.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you're looking for stolen goods, I can get my hands on plenty.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: What? Is this really how people see us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do your colleagues know what you've done? What do they think?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I... haven't had time to inform them yet. But I will! I just have to get my story straight...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So can anyone just go around stealing and call it archaeology?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: No, but this really is archaeology! I promise!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're supposed to protect history, not steal it!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Sometimes protecting history means taking... unconventional measures.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nobody wants to see stolen goods on display!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: That's why I put it in storage! No, wait, that's not what I meant!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You should give Varlamore a chance before stealing their stuff.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Maybe, but I know what I saw, and I wasn't impressed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you even have room to display this artefact?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: No, but our duties go well beyond putting artefacts on display for adventurers to gawk at!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't you have enough artefacts of your own?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: If everyone cared only for their own town's history, we'd have a pretty boring world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you really think you did the right thing?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I had no other choice. I couldn't just stand by!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you know Varlamore sends thieves to the Colosseum?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: But I'm no thief! Give me a chance to explain...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if I came into your house and stole your stuff?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Then you'd be a thief! This is nothing like that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've committed a crime.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I have not! Just give me a chance to explain!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How will Varlamorians learn their history now?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Well, they can always come visit. Or perhaps we can arrange a loan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Varlamore might never let you and your colleagues visit again!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I've done Varlamore a great service! They just don't know it yet...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You should return all these artefacts. It's the only right thing to do.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I'll return them as soon as I know they'll be safe. Until then, they'll stay right here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're a disgrace to your entire profession!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: How dare you! I've never once failed to follow the proper procedures. Well, except this one time. But this is different!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Varlamore might close their lands again after this.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Better that than the loss of a priceless artefact! It's not my fault they didn't look after it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you explain the other stolen artefacts you have in storage?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I didn't steal them! The museum simply borrowed them for further study.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You could have damaged the artefact!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Had I not done something, it could well have been damaged anyway!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why don't you just cooperate with Varlamore?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Believe me, I've been trying!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why should you get to decide who gets to keep their artefacts?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Because I'm the only one willing to protect them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're setting a terrible example.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Oh dear... Children! Pay no attention to this loud, dramatic argument!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is everything here stolen from other museums?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Nothing here is stolen from other museums! Er... I mean, nothing is stolen at all!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can't justify theft by saying it's for preservation.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Well that's the reality. Sometimes you have to make tough choices.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sick of your excuses!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I'm sick of your shouting!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not above the law! You stole this stuff!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: It's more complicated than that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What makes you think you know better?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: The fact that I had to save this artefact at all says enough. It needs to be kept safe!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if it's cursed?! It could be dangerous!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Are you quite serious? Just because an artefact is from another culture doesn't mean it's cursed. Adventurers these days...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stealing is stealing, no matter how you try to justify it.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Normally I'd agree, but this one situation is a little more nuanced than that...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is the artefact really safer here?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Absolutely! We're the greatest, most well-equipped museum in the world!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look me in the eye and tell me you did the right thing.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I... I did the right... Stop staring at me like that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're hoarding artefacts, but you should be sharing them!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: We don't have enough room to show everything in our collections. If only we had the funding...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're a loose cannon.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: A loose cannon? A wild card maybe. Or a lone ranger. But a loose cannon?! Surely not...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hand that artefact back this instant!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: There's no need to take that tone with me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess you were just in it for the glory, were you?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: That's not true! I mean, I am better than this. I mean... oh, I'm all mixed up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is stealing! Thieving! Taking what's not yours!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Please stop telling everyone I'm a thief...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've stolen from several other museums, too!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Not every museum has the resources to keep their artefacts safe. We're just taking care of them for now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This artefact doesn't belong to you! It belongs to Varlamore!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I believe artefacts like this belong to all of us. That's why it's so important for me to preserve them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you no respect for your colleagues in Varlamore?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: They had the choice to take better care with this artefact! That's not on me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't you embarrassed to have stolen items in your collection?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I'm not embarrassed! I just put it in storage for... for normal reasons!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're nothing but a common thief!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: How dare you? I'm a well-renowned professional, not a thief!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stealing is against the law!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Well, sometimes the law is wrong!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You ought to be ashamed.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: For what? I'm only doing my job!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This could destroy your reputation! Everyone will think you're a thief!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Oh, nonsense! Any real historian would see I'm only trying to preserve vital historical knowledge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've betrayed the trust of Varlamore.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: It's not my fault they couldn't look after their own history. Better that I took it than some thief!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you even a real historian, or just a petty criminal?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Of course I'm a real historian! This just isn't my finest moment...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't even have room to display it! It's just sitting in storage!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Artefacts aren't just for gawking at! We keep things in storage so they can be studied and taken care of.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not preserving anything. You're just a thief.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Pish-posh! I haven't dedicated my life to this museum for you to slander my good name!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Think of the Varlamorian children who won't get to see this artefact.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Now that's not true! They can see it any time they visit Varrock, for free!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can't just keep claiming these stolen goods as your own!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Oh, pish posh! I've had enough of this little shame game of yours! I've got better things to be doing with my time. Come back to me when you've got your facts straight!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I didn't steal anything! These are ethically acquired antiquities!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean? How did you come into possession of Xerna's Diadem? I've heard all about your travels back here with it.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Well...

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Naturally, as soon as Varlamore opened, I was keen to visit their Grand Museum.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: There was a big event over at the Colosseum, so it was nice and quiet. Perfect for taking in everything the museum had to offer.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: All of a sudden, I heard a strange noise coming from behind me...

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: It was a thief! He'd stolen Xerna's Diadem!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I had to act quick. I called upon my skills from my time in the Varrock Guard and I showed him what for!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: He went down easily, but I was left with a conundrum: What to do with the diadem.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: This was a priceless artefact, and it was at risk! I knew it had to be protected, and there was nowhere better than in Varrock...

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Now I don't know what to do...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can't just be taking these things as your own!

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I promise, I only wanted to keep it safe. I guess I panicked...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do you a deal. If you arrange for the safe delivery of the diadem back to the Grand Museum, I'll let them know it's taken care of without mentioning your name.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Okay... I'll have it sent back at once.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. I'll head over there and let them know.

sections.title: Talking to Curator Haig Halen after arranging the diadem's return

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Welcome to the museum of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About Xerna's Diadem...

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I'm just making arrangements to have it sent back to Fortis. It will be there very soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. I'll head over there and let them know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Curator Herminius
lines.text: Nilsal to you, iknami. Welcome back to the Grand Museum. If you have any questions, I'm happy to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that empty display...

lines.speaker: Curator Herminius
lines.text: How are you getting on with your investigation?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it turned out to be quite the saga, but I found it! Xerna's Diadem is on its way back here, as we speak.

lines.speaker: Curator Herminius
lines.text: It is? That's great news! Where did you find it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was a little far away... But we'll save that story for another day.

lines.speaker: Curator Herminius
lines.text: Well, what matters is that you found it. Now, I guess you'll be expecting that reward the display mentioned?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, I better make some space for it.

lines.speaker: Curator Herminius
lines.text: Well, what matters is that you found it. Now, I guess you'll be expecting that reward the display mentioned? Here you go.
